title: Breakpoints
text: Breakpoints A breakpoint is a marked code line, trigger, error, exception, or condition that forces the application or component to break and the debugger to take control of execution. Set breakpoints to make your code easier to debug, to notify you of a specific problem or occurrence, or because you expect to find a coding error or exception that occurred in a particular location. A single breakpoint placed in a suspected trouble spot allows the application to run uninterrupted until that point is reached. Multiple breakpoints allow you to break up your code into more manageable pieces for testing. Conditional breakpoints allow the application to run until specific conditions have been met. These conditions must be ones that the $condition ProcScript function can handle. Error or exception related breakpoints allow the debugger to break on all or specific errors and exceptions. For details, see Working with Error and Exception related breakpoints and Configuring the Debugger Error and Exception behavior . Tip: Add single breakpoints at various locations in your ProcScript, and then edit these breakpoints individually. Otherwise, you first need to know the exact location (ProcScript module, line number) and format expected by the debugger. Breakpoints constitute part of the debugger state. Related Links Debugger State The state of the Debugger consists of current settings, watches, and breakpoints. The current state can be saved as a named configuration for repeated use. Working with breakpoints Set breakpoints on ProcScript instructions, triggers, errors, or conditions. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
